macro_events.event_summary: US CPI inflation jumped to a 3-year high, with the report suggesting further price pressure ahead — pushing back the timeline for Fed rate cuts.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer rates / repricing of Fed cut expectations lifts discount rates
Real assets and pricing-power names outperform during sustained inflation
Long-duration equities and rate-sensitive sectors derate as Treasury yields rise

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated oil major with direct revenue leverage to energy prices, which are typically both a cause and beneficiary of CPI re-acceleration. Earnings rise dollar-for-dollar with crude/refined product prices.
Same energy pricing-power thesis as XOM; upstream production gives direct topline lift if commodity-driven inflation is part of the print.
Largest US-listed gold miner — gold is the classic hedge against sticky inflation and a Fed perceived behind the curve; operating leverage to gold price amplifies move.
Largest US bank by assets benefits from higher-for-longer rates via net interest margin expansion; asset-sensitive balance sheet reprices faster than deposits.
Exchange operator sees volume spike in interest-rate futures (SOFR, Treasury) and commodity contracts on rate-path repricing; direct revenue from clearing volumes.

losers.rationale: Realty Income — net lease REIT with bond-like cash flows and heavy debt stack; rises in 10Y yield directly compress AFFO multiple and increase refi costs.
Low-income consumer exposure and thin retail margins — sticky inflation erodes real wages of core customer and squeezes basket size; cost passthrough already maxed.

headlines: Inflation jumps to 3-year high, CPI shows, and that’s not the end of it

macro_events.event_summary: CNBC reports inflation is reaccelerating broadly — not just from Iran-related oil prices, but also in shelter, food, and other categories.
primary_drivers: Higher-for-longer rates outlook as reacceleration delays Fed cuts
Energy price pass-through to producers with pricing power
Real asset / inflation hedge rotation (commodities, energy, staples with pricing power)

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with direct upstream leverage to Iran-driven oil price spike; every $10/bbl typically adds ~$4-5B to annual upstream earnings.
Same direct crude price leverage as XOM; Permian-weighted upstream gains immediately on oil reacceleration.
Highest crude-price beta among large-cap E&Ps due to Permian pure-play exposure and leverage; benefits most per dollar of oil move.
Food inflation reacceleration boosts ag merchandising margins and grain trading spreads; direct beneficiary of higher food category prices cited in article.
Domestic steel producer with pricing power in inflationary regime; commodity producers historically outperform when CPI surprises hot.
Gold miner — reacceleration of broad inflation (not just energy) revives real-asset/inflation hedge bid; gold typically bid on sticky CPI prints.
Multifamily apartment REIT — shelter is explicitly called out as reaccelerating; rent growth directly flows to same-store NOI.
Self-storage with monthly repricing — fastest pass-through of shelter-adjacent inflation to revenue of any REIT subsector.

losers.rationale: Airline with high jet fuel cost exposure and limited hedging vs peers; Iran-driven crude spike directly compresses unit margins.
Jet fuel is ~25-30% of airline opex; Iran oil shock plus weakening consumer from inflation creates double squeeze on fares + costs.
Dollar General's low-income customer is most squeezed by reaccelerating food and shelter costs; ticket and traffic both pressured.
Homebuilder — sticky shelter inflation keeps mortgage rates elevated, depressing affordability and order volumes.
Auto and durables retailers face consumer wallet share loss as food/shelter/energy take a bigger CPI bite; discretionary big-ticket pressured.

headlines: It's not just Iran and oil raising inflation. Prices also are reaccelerating in these other areas

macro_events.event_summary: An Iran war is looming over Trump's diplomatic trip to China, raising geopolitical risk across both Middle East oil supply and US-China relations simultaneously.
primary_drivers: Middle East oil supply risk premium (Strait of Hormuz exposure)
US-China diplomatic uncertainty weighing on China-exposed names

beneficiaries.rationale: Largest US integrated oil major with diversified non-Iranian production; direct beneficiary of Brent/WTI risk premium from Iran conflict escalation. Every $10 Brent move flows materially to earnings.
Pure-play E&P with high leverage to crude prices and no Iranian/Hormuz operational exposure; benefits from supply-disruption risk premium without offsetting downside.
Prime contractor for THAAD, PAC-3 Patriot interceptors, and JASSM — exactly the systems consumed/replenished in an Iran conflict scenario. Direct munitions restocking beneficiary.
Makes Tomahawk cruise missiles, SM-3/SM-6 interceptors, and AMRAAM — core munitions used against Iranian missile/drone threats. Stockpile replenishment thesis.
B-2/B-21 bomber prime, integrated air defense, and munitions — direct exposure to any sustained Iran campaign and to elevated FY27 defense budget topline.
Cheniere is the largest US LNG exporter; Middle East shipping disruption (Hormuz/Bab-el-Mandeb) tightens global gas markets and lifts spot LNG prices, boosting marketing margins.
Drone/loitering munitions and target-drone supplier — Iran conflict accelerates demand for unmanned systems already a Pentagon priority.

losers.rationale: Highest jet-fuel sensitivity among US airlines with no fuel hedging program; Brent spike from Iran risk directly compresses margins. Also exposure to international route disruption.
Largest transpacific and Middle East/India route exposure among US carriers; faces both fuel cost spike and potential airspace closures/rerouting costs.
Domestic carrier with thin margins and limited hedging post-2024; pure fuel-cost hit with no offsetting premium-cabin pricing power.
Highest-profile US company with Shanghai Gigafactory dependency and Chinese consumer brand exposure; Trump-Xi trip disruption from Iran crisis raises tariff/retaliation overhang.
Macau gaming generates majority of revenue; US-China diplomatic deterioration risks reciprocal measures against US-listed gaming operators and visa/travel flows.
Cruise operator with Mediterranean and Middle East itineraries; Iran conflict forces costly rerouting and dampens booking curves for the region.
Roughly two-thirds of revenue tied to China handset OEMs; any breakdown in Trump's China trip raises export-control retaliation risk on its highest-margin business.

headlines: Analysis: Iran war hangs over Trump's China trip — and his presidency

macro_events.event_summary: Oil prices rise on renewed Middle East tensions as Trump says the US-Iran ceasefire is "on life support," raising risk of supply disruption.
primary_drivers: Geopolitical risk premium in crude (Iran supply/Strait of Hormuz risk)
Higher realized crude prices boost upstream margins
Defense procurement tailwind from escalation risk

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; earnings highly levered to Brent. Direct beneficiary of crude price spike with deep liquidity making it the default oil headline trade.
High-beta US E&P with Permian leverage; among the most rate-of-change sensitive large-caps to crude moves. Historically outperforms peers on geopolitical oil spikes.
E&P with direct Egypt/North Africa operating exposure; benefits both from higher Brent and from regional risk premium pricing into its assets.
Oilfield services name with historical Middle East revenue concentration; sustained higher crude supports activity/capex outlook from NOC customers.
Patriot/THAAD/missile defense prime contractor — direct beneficiary of escalation-driven munitions restock and allied FMS demand if Iran tensions worsen.
Raytheon makes interceptors (SM-3, SM-6, Patriot) directly consumed in Middle East air-defense engagements; replenishment orders are the mechanical link to escalation.
TechnipFMC — subsea equipment provider with significant Middle East/offshore backlog; higher sustained oil prices accelerate FID on projects.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; Delta lacks the structural hedging program some peers carry, making it the most crude-sensitive large US airline.
Same fuel cost pass-through dynamic as DAL with high international/long-haul exposure; rising crude directly compresses unit margins.
Southwest's fuel hedging book has been reduced post-2022; high fuel-cost-per-ASM sensitivity makes it a reflexive sell on oil spikes.
Carnival's bunker fuel is a major variable cost and Middle East/Red Sea routing risk adds insurance and rerouting costs on top of fuel price.
Express air network is jet-fuel intensive; fuel surcharges lag spot moves so near-term margin pressure if crude sustains higher.
Commodity chemicals producer with naphtha/ethane feedstock exposure; higher oil compresses cracker spreads on the naphtha side and pressures global polyethylene economics.

headlines: Oil prices rise as tensions flare in Middle East, Trump says US-Iran ceasefire is 'on life support'

macro_events.event_summary: White House economic advisor Hassett touts strong credit card spending while consumer delinquencies rise and farm bankruptcies surge 46%, signaling consumer credit stress and acute agricultural sector distress.
primary_drivers: Rising consumer credit delinquencies increasing loss provisions at card issuers
Farm sector bankruptcies hitting ag lenders, equipment makers, and input suppliers
Distressed-consumer trade-down behavior benefiting discount retailers

beneficiaries.rationale: Dollar General is a direct beneficiary of consumer trade-down as credit-stressed households shift spending to discount channels; same-store traffic historically inflects positively when delinquencies rise.
Dollar Tree captures the same trade-down flow from credit-constrained consumers; low-income cohort with rising card delinquencies is its core demographic.
Walmart grocery and general merchandise gains share during consumer stress episodes; management has repeatedly cited higher-income trade-in during prior delinquency cycles.
Enova specializes in subprime/non-prime installment and short-term lending; demand rises as prime card issuers tighten and stressed consumers seek alternative credit.

losers.rationale: Deere has direct revenue exposure to US farm capex; a 46% jump in farm bankruptcies signals collapsing replacement demand for ag equipment and rising used-equipment inventory pressuring new sales and margins.
AGCO is even more pure-play ag equipment than Deere with higher row-crop exposure; farm bankruptcy spike directly threatens FY order book and dealer financing health.
CNH Industrial's ag machinery segment faces the same farm-bankruptcy demand destruction as DE/AGCO with weaker balance sheet cushion.
Capital One has the highest subprime credit card mix among large issuers; rising delinquencies flow directly into NCO rates and reserve builds, compressing EPS.
Synchrony's private-label card book skews to lower-FICO retail customers; delinquency acceleration directly hits provisions and the stock's credit-cycle beta is among the highest in financials.
Ally's auto loan book overlaps with the same credit-stressed cohort seeing card delinquencies rise; subprime auto NCOs typically lag card NCOs by one quarter.
Mosaic sells potash/phosphate fertilizer directly into US row-crop farmers; farm bankruptcy surge signals weakening application rates and pricing power into next planting season.

headlines: Hassett brags that credit card spending is 'through the roof' — as delinquencies climb and farm bankruptcies jump 46%
